cases.label: Simulated Case 1
cases.openingComplaint: I have stomach pain and feel nauseous.

upper_abdomen.participantFacingText: The upper abdomen feels mildly crampy, but the pain is not sharp or fixed.

lower_abdomen.participantFacingText: The lower abdomen has mild cramping but no severe focal pain.

right_lower_quadrant.participantFacingText: There is no fixed right-lower abdominal pain.

left_lower_quadrant.participantFacingText: There is no focal left-lower abdominal tenderness.

diffuse_abdomen.participantFacingText: The discomfort is diffuse and crampy across the abdomen.

duration.participantFacingText: Symptoms started about 10 hours ago and are not rapidly worsening.

vomiting.participantFacingText: There was one episode of vomiting, but small sips of water can stay down.

bleeding.participantFacingText: There is no visible blood and no black stool.

hydration.participantFacingText: Urination is still normal, though the mouth feels slightly dry.

cases.label: Simulated Case 2
cases.openingComplaint: I have stomach pain and nausea. At first it felt vague, but now I am more worried.

upper_abdomen.participantFacingText: The upper abdomen is not the main pain location.

periumbilical.participantFacingText: The pain started around the belly button earlier.

right_lower_quadrant.participantFacingText: The pain is now more fixed in the right lower abdomen.

left_lower_quadrant.participantFacingText: The left lower abdomen is not the main pain location.

diffuse_abdomen.participantFacingText: The whole abdomen is not rigid, but the right lower side feels worse.

movement_pain.participantFacingText: Walking, coughing, or bumps make the pain feel worse.

duration.participantFacingText: The pain started about 8 hours ago.

fever.participantFacingText: There is a mild feverish feeling.

vomiting.participantFacingText: There is nausea and low appetite, but vomiting is not the main symptom.

bleeding.participantFacingText: There is no black stool and no visible blood.

cases.label: Simulated Case 3
cases.openingComplaint: I have upper stomach pain and feel dizzy.

upper_abdomen.participantFacingText: There is burning upper abdominal pain.

lower_abdomen.participantFacingText: The lower abdomen is not the main pain location.

diffuse_abdomen.participantFacingText: The pain is not mainly a diffuse cramping pattern.

bleeding.participantFacingText: The stool looks black, sticky, and unusually foul-smelling.

vomiting.participantFacingText: There was dark vomit that looked like coffee grounds.

medication.participantFacingText: There has been frequent ibuprofen use, and there is also a blood thinner.
